algorithms.title: Respiratory Complaint Differential Sequence
algorithms.description: A stepwise approach to narrowing the cause of a respiratory complaint while supporting oxygenation throughout.
steps: Apply supplemental oxygen and continuous pulse oximetry regardless of cause — hypoxia is a common thread.
Assess for wheezing: if present and not improved by home rescue therapy, give nebulized beta-2 agonists.
Watch for a silent chest in a wheezing patient — this signals WORSENING obstruction, not improvement; escalate care.
Assess rate and depth: very slow, shallow breathing (bradypnea) in a calm-appearing patient indicates respiratory failure — assist ventilation immediately.
Consider spontaneous pneumothorax in a COPD patient with sudden dyspnea, pleuritic pain, and diminished unilateral breath sounds.
Consider pulmonary embolism in sudden dyspnea with pleuritic pain, tachycardia, and clot risk factors (immobility, surgery, malignancy).
Screen for sepsis (fever, altered LOC) as an underlying cause of a respiratory presentation, especially with a possible pneumonia source.
Reassess frequently and escalate ventilatory support as needed.
